phase1.explanation:
Static members belong to the CLASS, not instances:

Static variables (class variables):
• Shared across ALL objects
• Only one copy exists
• Declared with 'static'

Instance variables: 
• Each object has its own copy
• Not static

Static methods:
• Called on class: ClassName.method()
• Can't access instance members directly
• Can't use 'this'

Static block:
• Runs when class loads (once)
• Used for initialization
• Before main() runs

static final constants:
• Common pattern: public static final double PI = 3.14159;
• Can't be changed

Math class: all members are static! (utility class)

When to use static:
• Utility methods (Math.sqrt, Collections.sort)
• Constants
• Shared state (counters, configuration)

phase2.errorMessage:
Counter.java:8: error: non-static variable instanceValue cannot be referenced from a static context
        instanceValue = 10;  // ERROR!
        ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Static methods can only access static members directly!

instanceValue is an instance variable — it belongs to an object. Since static methods don't have a 'this' reference, they can't access instance members.

Fixes:
1. Make instanceValue static (if it should be shared)
2. Access through an object: obj.instanceValue
3. Don't access instance variables from static methods

Remember: static context → only static members!

phase3.prompt:
Create a Student class with:
• Instance fields: name (String), grade (int)
• Static field: schoolName (String) — shared by all students
• Static method: setSchoolName(String name) — sets the school name
• Constructor: sets name and grade
• Method: displayInfo() — prints all info

In main, create 3 students, set school name once, and display all.
dialogueHints: schoolName is static — shared across all students
setSchoolName is static — called on the class (Student.setSchoolName(...))
displayInfo should be instance method (prints name, grade, schoolName)
